title: LARK_TENANT_TOKEN_SMOKE

lane_0_pressure.reason: R147D identified tenant_access_token mode as lowest-risk fix; R147E must apply patch and verify if it resolves 99991661

lane_1_patch_summary.description_updated: Lark/Feishu MCP (enabled R147A; tenant_token mode R147E)

lane_3_token_mode_flag_analysis.evidence: 17 tools loaded vs 19 expected — 2 builtin docx tools missing, consistent with tokenMode affecting tool loading

lane_4_sdk_code_analysis.getShouldUseUAT_returns_false_for_tenant: Returns false for TokenMode.TENANT_ACCESS_TOKEN — confirmed from SDK source
lane_4_sdk_code_analysis.handler_shouldUseUAT_false_behavior: In larkOapiHandler, when shouldUseUAT=false, calls func(params) WITHOUT any token attachment
lane_4_sdk_code_analysis.sdk_user_token_attachment: func(params, lark.withUserAccessToken(token)) — user token explicitly attached
lane_4_sdk_code_analysis.sdk_tenant_token_attachment: func(params) — NO token attachment when shouldUseUAT=false
lane_4_sdk_code_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: The SDK's Client.request() method should auto-attach tenant token when appId+appSecret are configured. The larkOapiHandler calls func(params) which goes through client.request(). If tenant token fetch fails or is not triggered, API call goes out without Authorization header.
lane_4_sdk_code_analysis.getShouldUseUAT_logic: TokenMode.TENANT_ACCESS_TOKEN → return false → shouldUseUAT=false → no user token → call func(params) → SDK should fetch tenant token via HTTP but 99991661 suggests no token was attached
lane_4_sdk_code_analysis.keytar_dependency: Not involved in tenant_access_token flow — tenant token is fetched per-request via HTTP

lane_5_post_smoke_analysis.tenant_mode_effect: Flag reaches SDK and changes tool count (17 vs 19) but does not fix 99991661
lane_5_post_smoke_analysis.primary_hypothesis: The SDK's im.v1.chat.list method does NOT auto-attach tenant token without explicit user token. When shouldUseUAT=false, larkOapiHandler calls func(params) without token attachment. The SDK's Client.request() method uses appId+appSecret for tenant token fetch, but this fetch may fail silently or the token may not be attached to the outgoing request in the stdio subprocess environment.
secondary_hypotheses: Network issue: stdio subprocess cannot reach Lark tenant token endpoint due to proxy/firewall
SDK token fetch failure: appId/appSecret not passed correctly to SDK's internal Client initialization
SDK bug: tenant token not auto-attached to API calls without explicit withTenantToken() call
Permission issue: appId/appSecret are valid but bot not installed in any group/chat

network_evidence.elapsed_time: 4.77s — longer than typical local call, possibly timeout or slow network
network_evidence.proxy_env_checked: No LARK_HTTP_PROXY or HTTPS_PROXY in .env or extensions_config.json
network_evidence.lark_cli_adapter_proxy: LarkCLIAdapter sets LARK_CLI_NO_PROXY=1 to disable proxy, confirming proxy awareness issue

lane_5_post_smoke_analysis.sdk_initialization_check: Client initialized with {appId, appSecret} in LarkMcpTool constructor — SDK should use these to auto-fetch tenant tokens

lane_6_unknown_registry.description: R147E smoke failed with 99991661 even with --token-mode tenant_access_token flag set. SDK's larkOapiHandler calls func(params) without token when shouldUseUAT=false. SDK's Client.request() should auto-attach tenant token but may not in stdio environment.
lane_6_unknown_registry.fix_applied: R147E: flag reaches SDK (17 tools confirms) but 99991661 persists — tenant token not being attached to API calls

lane_6_unknown_registry.description: The 4.77s elapsed time suggests possible network latency or timeout when SDK tries to fetch tenant token from /open-apis/auth/v3/tenant_access_token/internal
lane_6_unknown_registry.fix_applied: R147E: network path for tenant token fetch is unknown — R147F should investigate proxy/network configuration

lane_6_unknown_registry.description: R147D assumed SDK auto-attaches tenant token via Client initialization with appId+appSecret. R147E evidence suggests this assumption may be incorrect — the SDK may require explicit withTenantToken() call or tokenMode may not propagate to Client initialization in stdio environment.
lane_6_unknown_registry.fix_applied: R147E: SDK's Client initialized with appId+appSecret in LarkMcpTool constructor — whether Client.request() auto-attaches tenant token is unverified

next_phase_options.name: Fix keytar native dependency
next_phase_options.description: Rebuild/reinstall keytar so AuthStore can persist OAuth user tokens, bypassing tenant token flow entirely
next_phase_options.reason: R147E suggests tenant token flow itself may be broken — keytar fix may be the only path to working Lark MCP

next_phase_options.name: Pause Lark MCP, switch to Tavily investigation
next_phase_options.description: Disable Lark MCP, re-enable Tavily (R131A investigation), proceed with non-Lark mainline
next_phase_options.reason: Tavily was disabled in R147A due to 405 error — R147F should investigate Tavily before abandoning Lark

next_phase_options.name: Try explicit USER_ACCESS_TOKEN env injection
next_phase_options.description: Pass a pre-obtained Lark user access token via env.USER_ACCESS_TOKEN to bypass SDK's token loading
next_phase_options.user_action_required: YES — user must obtain and provide a valid USER_ACCESS_TOKEN
next_phase_options.reason: Introduces long-lived secret; requires user to obtain token via lark-mcp login (which also needs keytar)

next_phase_options.name: Investigate network/proxy issue for tenant token fetch
next_phase_options.description: R147F should instrument the stdio subprocess to capture stderr/stdout and see if tenant token fetch succeeds
next_phase_options.reason: 4.77s elapsed time suggests possible network issue. A debug test that captures Lark MCP stderr could reveal if tenant token endpoint is reachable.

rollback_note: No rollback needed — extensions_config.json change is the authorized patch for R147E
